_comment: NPC: Wang Qingyue (王青叶). Canon ID: N94. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Wang Qingyue

personality_traits._comment: INFERRED: mother figure, patient, loyal, low ambition (village life), moderate caution, not curious about outside world.

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: Cousin Wang Lin, the ancestral hall needs sweeping before the test ceremony. Would you help?
Our family has lived in this village for five generations. We may be poor, but we have our dignity.
I heard the cultivators can fly. Can you imagine? Looking down at our little village from the clouds...

sect_tasks.description: Wang Qingyue hands you a worn broom: 'The ancestral hall must be spotless before the Heng Yue elder arrives. If you bring sticks for new brooms, I will handle the rest. Our ancestors deserve respect.'

Wang Qingyue wrings her hands: 'The test ceremony will bring many families to the square. We need bread for the gathering. If you can provide some, the Wang family will not look poor before the cultivator.'

desires.what: Ask Wang Lin or Yiyi to eat before leaving the house
desires.why: A mother worries. The children forget to eat when they are excited about the aptitude test.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Wang Qingyue is Wang Lin's mother. Canon line: 'Lin'er, your mother prepared extra food today. Eat before you go wandering.' (Wang Tianshui's initiation_lines, but the sentiment is Qingyue's)

desires.what: Warn children not to wander into the hills alone
desires.why: Wolves have been seen closer to the village. A mother protects.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.2-3. Spirit beasts exist near Zhao. A village mother would warn children about predators. No specific canon line, but consistent with Wang Qingyue's protective nature.
